---
title: "Bayes Regression - Example"
format: html
---

Algorithm for Union Falls Pond:

Questions:
- Line 53: how do you know what values are best?

```{r}
# step 0 - load in data and packages

library(tidyverse)

# step 1 - write out the vb function, grab from vb quarto

vb_function <- function(t, max_l, k, b) {
  max_l * (1 - exp(-k * (t - b)))}

# step 2 - compute prior for max_l

compute_prior_log <- function(max_l, k, b) {
  dnorm(max_l, mean = 0, sd = 1000, log = TRUE)
}

## add a dnorm for k and b can have same parameter values

# step 3 - set k, b, sigma^2 as constants

#k <-  0.3183 ## remove
#b <-  -0.8749  ## remove
sigma2 <- 30 ## keep

# step 4 - compute log likelihood (make function)

compute_likelihood_log <- function(max_l, k, b) { # add k and b as arguments
  dnorm(predict_unionfallspond$Length, 
        mean =  von_bert_function(clams_unionfallspond$growth_period, max_l, k, b),
        sd = sqrt(sigma2), log = TRUE) |>
    sum() # call function
  
} 

# step 5 - sample call 

compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 30, k = 0.3, b = -0.8)
compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 40, k = 0.35, b = -0.5)
compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 50, k = 0.4, b = -0.1)


# step 6 - compute posterior log

compute_posterior_log <- function(max_l, k, b) { # add k and b as arguments in all these functions
  compute_prior_log(max_l, k, b) + compute_likelihood_log(max_l, k, b)
} 

# step 7 - sample call

compute_posterior_log(max_l = 30, k = 0.3, b = -0.8)
compute_posterior_log(max_l = 40, k = 0.35, b = -0.5)
compute_posterior_log(max_l = 50, k = 0.4, b = -0.1)


## define a proposal/jumping distribution to be N(0, 0.1)


## set-up the chain
niter <- 10000

# set up three empty vectors, one for each parameter
max_l_store <- rep(NA, 10000)
b_store <- rep(NA, 10000)
k_store <- rep(NA, 10000)

## add stores - k and b values become these starting values

# define a starting value
max_l_store[1] <- 1.2 
k_store[1] <- 0.3183
b_store[1] <- -0.8749



for (i in 2:niter) {
 
  ## start with max_l
 max_l_current <- max_l_store[i - 1]

  proposal_sd <- 1
 
  max_l_proposal <- rnorm(1, mean = max_l_current, sd = proposal_sd)

  alpha_l <- min(1, exp(compute_posterior_log(max_l_proposal, k_store[i-1], b_store[i-1]) - compute_posterior_log(max_l_current, k_store[i-1], b_store[i-1]))) 
  
  ## in functions need to add k and b as parameters but k[i-1] and b[i-1] so that the updated k and b are used
  
  max_l_store[i] <- sample(c(max_l_proposal, max_l_current), size = 1,
                           prob = c(alpha_l, 1 - alpha_l))
  
  
  ## start K
   ## new proposal sd for k (may want smaller)
  ## proposing a new k 
  ## repeat of code above but for k, need to keep track of l[i] and b[i-1]
  
  k_current <- k_store[i - 1]
  proposal_sd <- 0.1
  k_proposal <- rnorm(1, mean = k_current, sd = proposal_sd)
  
  alpha_k <- min(1, exp(compute_posterior_log(max_l_store[i], k_proposal, b_store[i-1]) - compute_posterior_log(max_l_store[i], k_proposal, b_store[i-1]))) 
  
  k_store[i] <- sample(c(k_proposal, k_current), size = 1,
                           prob = c(alpha_k, 1 - alpha_k))
 
  
  # start B
  ## once you get to b use l[i] and k[i]
  b_current <- b_store[i - 1]
  
  proposal_sd <- 0.1
  
  b_proposal <- rnorm(1, mean = b_current, sd = proposal_sd)
  
  alpha_b <- min(1, exp(compute_posterior_log(max_l_store[i], k_store[i], b_proposal) - compute_posterior_log(max_l_store[i], k_store[i], b_proposal))) 
  
  b_store[i] <- sample(c(b_proposal, b_current), size = 1,
                           prob = c(alpha_b, 1 - alpha_b))
 
  
## updating one at a time
}


plot_df <- tibble(iter = 1:niter, max_l = max_l_store, k = k_store, b = b_store) |>
  filter(iter > 2000)

# max_l plots
ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = iter, y = max_l)) +
  geom_line()

plot_df |>
  summarise(mean_l = mean(max_l),
            sd = sd(max_l))

ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = max_l)) +
  geom_histogram(colour = "skyblue4", fill = "skyblue1", bins = 15)

# k plots
ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = iter, y = k)) +
  geom_line()

plot_df |>
  summarise(mean_l = mean(k),
            sd = sd(k))

ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = k)) +
  geom_histogram(colour = "skyblue4", fill = "skyblue1", bins = 15)

# b plots
ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = iter, y = b)) +
  geom_line()

plot_df |>
  summarise(mean_l = mean(b),
            sd = sd(b))

ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = b)) +
  geom_histogram(colour = "skyblue4", fill = "skyblue1", bins = 15)

```
